channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: SA Rugby Addresses Ticket Pricing Concerns Amid Sustainability Focus
item.description: SA Rugby president Mark Alexander has defended the organisation's decision to maintain high ticket prices for international matches, stating that it is a necessary measure for long-term sustainability...

item.title: Brumbies Finalize Triston Reilly Deal as New Squad Shaped
item.description: The ACT Brumbies are poised to announce Triston Reilly as their final recruit for the upcoming season, following his departure from the NSW Waratahs. The 27-year-old player, who has previously played ...

item.title: Spain Defeats France 2-0 to Reach FIFA World Cup Final
item.description: Spain secured a 2-0 semifinal victory over favored France in the 2026 FIFA World Cup, advancing to their second-ever final. Mikel Oyarzabal opened the scoring in the 22nd minute with a penalty, and Pe...

item.title: MTN empowers Nigerian youths through innovation and collaboration in Kano
item.description: MTN Nigeria has expanded its youth engagement initiative, The Gathering 100, to Kano, creating a platform for young innovators, entrepreneurs, and creatives to collaborate and showcase ideas. The even...

item.title: NITDA pushes for digital integration in Nigeria's infrastructure projects to boost economic growth
item.description: The National Information Technology Development Agency (NITDA) is advocating for the integration of digital components into all new infrastructure projects in Nigeria to support industrialization and ...

item.title: Pudukottai farmer leverages digital tools and eco-friendly methods for agricultural success
item.description: S. Muthulakshmi, a 50-year-old paddy farmer from Thirumalairayasamuthiram village in Pudukkottai District, has transformed her farming practices through training by the M.S. Swaminathan Research Found...

item.title: A mother's 17-year quest for justice after her murder in Crete
item.description: Jean Hanlon, a 53-year-old woman from Dumfries, Scotland, moved to Crete in 2003, where she built a new life. Her murder in 2009 remained unsolved for 17 years until her killer was finally convicted. ...

item.title: Criticism of a Hypothetical Question to Politicians
item.description: The article critiques the practice of journalists asking politicians to 'rule out' hypothetical scenarios, such as governing with One Nation, which the author deems a trivial and inane question. It re...

item.title: Catholic Bishop Suspends Priests for Misconduct and Issues New Church Directives
item.description: Bishop Hieronymus Emusugut Joya of Maralal has suspended six priests over allegations of adultery, abuse of authority, and mismanagement of church property. The bishop warned women against tempting pr...

item.title: Philippine IT-BPM Sector Projects $50.5 Billion Revenue by 2028 with AI Integration
item.description: The Philippine IT-BPM sector is projected to generate up to $50.5 billion in revenue by 2028, driven by the integration of artificial intelligence (AI) technologies. This growth is attributed to the i...

item.title: Jennifer Winget Marries in Celebrated Ceremony
item.description: Jennifer Winget, the popular Indian television actress known for her roles in shows like 'Kasauti Zindagi Ka' and 'Pyaar Ka Punchnama', recently tied the knot in a lavish wedding ceremony. The event, ...

item.title: AI's Role in Album Covers: Balancing Creativity and Collaboration
item.description: The article explores the growing influence of artificial intelligence in creating album cover art within African music culture. While AI can rapidly generate polished visuals, industry professionals a...

item.title: Bahati's Daughter Attends Prestigious School with High Fees
item.description: Gospel musician Bahati's daughter, Mueni, attends Good Testimony Schools, a reputable Nairobi institution founded in 1991. The school charges between KSh 60,000 and KSh 70,000 per term for tuition, wi...

item.title: Sara Duterte Case Involves National Security Concerns
item.description: The Philippine Palace has asserted that the allegations against Vice President Sara Duterte transcend a simple grave threats case, framing it as a matter of national security due to the involvement of...

item.title: Calgary massage therapist faces sexual assault charges after alleged incident in Glendale
item.description: A 52-year-old man has been charged with sexually assaulting a client at Kinesio Massage Therapy in Calgary's Glendale neighborhood. The charge stems from an allegation that the therapist touched the c...

item.title: Didier Deschamps' Tactical Shift and France's World Cup Semi-Final Defeat
item.description: The article analyses Didier Deschamps' tactical approach during France's World Cup semi-final against Spain, highlighting the tension between his cautious strategies and the team's attacking potential...

item.title: Muslim Man Stabbed in Utah Over Religion: Police Investigate
item.description: A Muslim man was severely injured after being stabbed multiple times by a suspect in Utah, USA, who targeted him due to his religious beliefs. The incident occurred at the Valley Fair Mall in West Val...

item.title: Maguire addresses disagreement with Hunt over reserve grade team
item.description: Michael Maguire, the coach of the South Sydney Rabbitohs, has addressed reports of a disagreement with assistant coach Karmichael Hunt regarding the need for a reserve grade side. The discussion arose...

item.title: Kenya Investigates Sh790 Million Theft Amid Public Anticipation
item.description: Kenyan authorities are still probing an alleged theft of Sh790 million, with the public eagerly awaiting a formal report. The case has gained significant attention due to the large sum involved and th...

item.title: China's economic growth slows to 4.3% in second quarter
item.description: China's economy grew at a 4.3% annualized pace in the April-June quarter, marking a slowdown from the 5% growth recorded in the first three months of the year. Despite strong exports driven by artific...
